message:
feat(docs): TOKIO-REPLACE T1 baseline — 9 planning docs + TLS/conformance fixes

Complete T1 baseline track for the Tokio ecosystem replacement program:

- T1.1.a: Capability inventory (28 families, 13 gaps identified)
- T1.1.b: Evidence map (test counts, module paths, formal proofs)
- T1.1.c: Risk register (R1-R4 ratings, priority formula, heat map)
- T1.2.a: Functional parity contracts (C01-C28 MUST/SHOULD/MAY)
- T1.2.c: Evidence checklist (E1-E8 categories, sign-off protocol)
- T1.3.b: Gap wave classification (13 gaps scored, 3 execution waves)
- T1.3.c: Dependency-aware roadmap (5 phases, 6 milestones, agent allocation)

TLS fixes: wall_clock_now→timeout_now, cfg stub signature, alpn_http docs.
Conformance: IO-004 doc comment accuracy (sequential, not concurrent).

Beads closed: 2oh2u.1.1.1–3, 1.2.1, 1.2.5, 1.3.1–3, epics 1.1–1.3.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+# Tokio Capability Risk Register
+
+**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.1.3` ([T1.1.c])
+**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
+**Date**: 2026-03-03
+**Parent inventory**: `docs/tokio_ecosystem_capability_inventory.md` (T1.1.a)
+**Parent evidence map**: `docs/tokio_capability_evidence_map.md` (T1.1.b)
+**Purpose**: Assign objective maturity and risk levels per capability family.
+This document is the baseline risk register for TOKIO-REPLACE execution planning.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Risk Assessment Methodology
+
+### 1.1 Maturity Level (M)
+
+| Level | Code | Criteria |
+|-------|------|----------|
+| Mature | M4 | Stable API, audited, 200+ tests, no open bugs |
+| Active | M3 | Functional API, 50+ tests, minor gaps |
+| Early | M2 | Core path works, API unstable, <50 tests or known gaps |
+| Parked | M1 | Scaffolding only or work paused |
+| Planned | M0 | No code, tracked in beads only |
+
+### 1.2 Replacement Risk (R)
+
+| Level | Code | Criteria |
+|-------|------|----------|
+| Low | R1 | Complete parity, well-tested, low interop friction |
+| Medium | R2 | Active parity with known minor gaps, moderate test coverage |
+| High | R3 | Partial parity, significant gaps, or high interop friction |
+| Critical | R4 | Blocking gaps for replacement claim, or no implementation |
+
+### 1.3 Determinism Score (D)
+
+| Level | Code | Criteria |
+|-------|------|----------|
+| Full | D3 | Fully deterministic in LabRuntime |
+| Partial | D2 | Core logic deterministic, I/O paths real-time |
+| None | D1 | Not wired into lab infrastructure |
+
+### 1.4 Composite Risk Priority
+
+```
+Priority = R × (5 - M) × (4 - D)
+```
+
+Higher values indicate higher urgency for remediation.
+
+---
+
+## 2. Risk Register
+
+| ID | Family | Maturity | Risk | Determ. | Tests | Priority | Blockers / Key Risks |
+|----|--------|----------|------|---------|-------|----------|---------------------|
+| F01 | Core Runtime | M4 | R1 | D3 | 1,100 | 1 | None. Fully proven, heavily tested. |
+| F02 | Structured Concurrency | M4 | R1 | D3 | 1,070 | 1 | None. 6/6 invariants Lean-proven. Asupersync advantage. |
+| F03 | Channels | M4 | R1 | D3 | 345 | 1 | None. Two-phase reserve/commit exceeds Tokio. |
+| F04 | Sync Primitives | M4 | R1 | D3 | 295 | 1 | None. All six Tokio primitives covered + extras. |

+# Tokio Ecosystem Capability Inventory
+
+**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.1.1` ([T1.1.a])
+**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
+**Date**: 2026-03-03
+**Purpose**: Definitive inventory of capability families required for a truthful
+full Tokio-ecosystem replacement claim.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Methodology
+
+This inventory enumerates every capability family in the Tokio ecosystem that a
+production Rust application might depend on, then maps it to the corresponding
+Asupersync surface. Each family is classified on four axes:
+
+- **Ownership boundary**: Where the capability lives in Asupersync's crate/feature topology
+  - `core` — always compiled, no feature gate
+  - `feature-gated` — behind a Cargo feature flag in the main crate
+  - `companion` — separate workspace crate (e.g., `frankenlab`, `conformance`)
+  - `out-of-scope` — intentionally excluded from replacement scope
+- **Parity status**: How complete the Asupersync surface is relative to Tokio's
+  - `complete` — API-equivalent or semantically-superior coverage
+  - `active` — substantial coverage, gaps in edge-case APIs
+  - `partial` — core path works, secondary APIs missing
+  - `early` — scaffolding exists, not production-ready
+  - `planned` — tracked in beads, no code yet
+  - `adapter` — interop via shim/bridge, not native
+- **Maturity**: Production readiness
+  - `mature` — tested, audited, stable API
+  - `active` — functional, API may evolve
+  - `early` — prototype, expect changes
+  - `parked` — work paused pending dependency resolution
+- **Determinism**: Lab runtime compatibility
+  - `strong` — fully deterministic under LabRuntime
+  - `mixed` — core paths deterministic, I/O paths real-time
+  - `none` — not yet wired into lab infrastructure
+

+## 4. Sign-Off Protocol

+### 4.1 Individual Gap Closure
+

+2. Implementer creates a sign-off bead comment listing all evidence by category
+3. Evidence is verified (tests pass in CI, benchmarks meet thresholds)

+5. Gap bead is closed with sign-off record

+### 4.2 Evidence Format
+

+
+### E2 — Cancellation Tests

+

+

+### 4.3 Waiver Policy
+
+If a mandatory evidence item cannot be produced:
+
+1. Document the specific item and reason it cannot be produced
+2. Assess risk impact (does absence increase migration risk?)
+3. Record compensating control (alternative evidence or planned follow-up)
+4. Obtain explicit approval from project owner
+5. Track waiver in gap bead with `waiver` label
+
+### 4.4 Failure Policy
+
+| Condition | Action |
+|-----------|--------|
+| Any E1 test fails | Gap remains open; fix and re-verify |
+| E2 oracle fails | Gap remains open; cancellation bug |
+| E3 replay diverges | Gap remains open; non-determinism bug |
+| E5 benchmark exceeds hard ceiling | Gap remains open; performance regression |
+| E6/E7 missing | Gap may close with `docs-pending` label; 7-day SLA |
+| E8 missing | Gap may close with `review-pending` label; 3-day SLA |

+# Tokio Functional Parity Contracts
+
+**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.2.1` ([T1.2.a])
+**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
+**Date**: 2026-03-03
+**Dependencies**: T1.1.a (inventory), T1.1.b (evidence), T1.1.c (risk)
+**Purpose**: Domain-specific functional contracts describing required behavior
+for Tokio-ecosystem parity. Each contract is precise enough to convert directly
+into conformance tests.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Contract Format
+
+Each capability domain receives a contract with:
+
+- **MUST** requirements: mandatory for parity claim
+- **SHOULD** requirements: important for production credibility
+- **MAY** requirements: nice-to-have, not blocking
+- **Asupersync-specific**: behaviors that differ from Tokio by design
+
+Contracts use verifiable language: "the implementation MUST return X when Y"
+rather than "should generally handle Z."
+
+---
+
+## 2. Domain Contracts
+
+### C01 — Core Runtime
+
+**Scope**: Task spawning, work-stealing scheduler, blocking pool, shutdown.
+
+| # | Req | Contract |
+|---|-----|----------|
+| C01.1 | MUST | `spawn()` returns a `TaskHandle` that can be awaited or cancelled |
+| C01.2 | MUST | Work-stealing scheduler distributes tasks across worker threads |
+| C01.3 | MUST | `spawn_blocking()` offloads closure to dedicated thread pool; does not block async workers |
+| C01.4 | MUST | Runtime shutdown drains all pending tasks with bounded cleanup budget |
+| C01.5 | MUST | Panics in spawned tasks are captured as `Outcome::Panicked`, not propagated to runtime |
+| C01.6 | SHOULD | Thread count is configurable via builder and environment variables |
+| C01.7 | SHOULD | Worker thread parking/unparking is adaptive (no busy-wait or excessive sleep) |
+| C01.8 | Asupersync | All spawned tasks are region-owned; orphan tasks are structurally impossible |
+| C01.9 | Asupersync | `Cx` capability context is required for spawn; no ambient `Handle::current()` |
+
+### C02 — Structured Concurrency and Cancellation
+
+**Scope**: Regions, scopes, cancellation protocol, obligations.
+
+| # | Req | Contract |
+|---|-----|----------|
+| C02.1 | MUST | Region close blocks until all owned tasks reach terminal state (quiescence) |
+| C02.2 | MUST | Cancellation is a three-phase protocol: request → drain → finalize |
+| C02.3 | MUST | Cancel requests are idempotent; repeated requests can only strengthen (never weaken) severity |
+| C02.4 | MUST | Parent cancellation propagates to all child regions and tasks |
+| C02.5 | MUST | Race losers receive `CancelKind::RaceLost` and drain correctly |
+| C02.6 | MUST | Obligation leak check detects unfulfilled obligations at region close |
+| C02.7 | MUST | `checkpoint()` returns `Err(Cancelled)` when cancel is requested and mask is zero |
+| C02.8 | MUST | `mask()` defers cancellation; checkpoint succeeds while masked |
+| C02.9 | Asupersync | No Tokio equivalent exists; these are Asupersync invariants, not parity requirements |
+
+### C03 — Channels
+
+**Scope**: mpsc, oneshot, broadcast, watch.
+
+| # | Req | Contract |
+|---|-----|----------|
+| C03.1 | MUST | Bounded mpsc: `send()` blocks when buffer is full; `recv()` blocks when empty |
+| C03.2 | MUST | Unbounded mpsc: `send()` never blocks; `recv()` blocks when empty |
+| C03.3 | MUST | Oneshot: single value delivery; sender drop causes `RecvError::Closed` |

+# Gap Scoring Results and Execution Wave Classification
+
+**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.1.3.2` ([T1.3.b])
+**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
+**Date**: 2026-03-03
+**Dependencies**: T1.3.a (scoring rubric), T1.1.c (risk register), T1.1.a (inventory)
+**Purpose**: Apply the gap severity rubric to every unresolved gap, assign execution
+waves, and record confidence levels and re-scoring assumptions.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Scoring Methodology
+
+Scores use the 6-dimension weighted model from T1.3.a:
+
+```
+Score = (U × 0.25) + (B × 0.25) + (S × 0.20) + (C × 0.10) + (E × 0.10) + (M × 0.10)
+```
+
+Each dimension rated 1-5. Composite range: 1.0–5.0.
+
+**Confidence levels**:
+- **High (H)**: Based on direct code audit, test counts, and API surface review
+- **Medium (M)**: Based on documented evidence + inference from related families
+- **Low (L)**: Based on assumptions that need validation
+
+---
+
+## 2. Detailed Per-Gap Scoring
+
+### G1 — QUIC Transport Completion
+
+| Dim | Score | Confidence | Justification |
+|-----|-------|------------|---------------|
+| U | 2 | H | <20% of production Rust apps use QUIC today (crates.io download data) |
+| B | 3 | M | Significant for full-replacement claim but HTTP/1.1+2 covers majority |
+| S | 3 | M | Incomplete loss recovery (RFC 9002) could cause data retransmission failures |
+| C | 3 | H | HTTP/3 (G2) depends on G1; also affects transport abstraction layer |
+| E | 1 | H | >4 weeks: native QUIC rewrite, congestion control, loss recovery |
+| M | 4 | H | Unblocks G2 (HTTP/3), QUIC-based messaging, multipath transport |
+
+**Composite**: (2×0.25) + (3×0.25) + (3×0.20) + (3×0.10) + (1×0.10) + (4×0.10) = **2.55 (P2)**
+
+**Assumptions that could change score**:
+- If Cloudflare/AWS mandate QUIC for new APIs → U jumps to 4, score → 3.55 (P1)
+- If quinn upstream drops Tokio hard dep → E jumps to 3 (fork + adapt), score → 2.75
+- If HTTP/3 adoption exceeds 30% in target workloads → B jumps to 4, score → 2.80
+
+**Mapped track**: T4 (QUIC/H3/advanced networking)
+**Risk register**: F15 — R4 (Critical), M1 (Parked), D1 (No determinism)
+

+| **Opportunistic** | Score < 2.0 OR conditional on other gaps | Best-effort, batch with related work |

+These gaps must resolve before any credible replacement claim. Execute in
+dependency order.

+
+G3 (Tokio interop layer) has a unique property: its success may **eliminate**
+multiple downstream gaps entirely. If the compatibility layer works well enough
+that users can run reqwest, sqlx, bb8, deadpool, and lapin against the
+Asupersync runtime:

+- G4 → eliminated (reqwest works directly)
+- G5 → eliminated (lapin works directly)
+- G9 → reduced to polish (bb8/deadpool work, native pool becomes nice-to-have)
+- G10 → eliminated (sqlx macros work directly)

+This means the **effective** gap count could drop from 13 to 8 after G3,
+making G3 the single highest-leverage investment in the entire program.

+
+| Milestone | Decision | If Yes | If No |
+|-----------|----------|--------|-------|
+| M2 | Does interop eliminate G4, G5, G9, G10? | Drop those gaps, reallocate agents | Build native implementations |

+| M4 | Are all P1 gaps closed? | Proceed to Phase D | Extend Phase C, add agents |
+| M5 | Does readiness gate pass? | Start Phase E | Fix blockers, re-run gate |

+## 5. Agent Allocation Strategy
+
+### Recommended Agent-to-Track Mapping
+
+| Agent Count | Track Assignment |
+|-------------|-----------------|
+| 1-2 agents | T7 (interop — critical path), T4 (QUIC — long lead) |
+| 3-4 agents | Add T5 (web/gRPC), T6 (DB/messaging) |
+| 5-6 agents | Add T2 (I/O), T3 (filesystem) |
+| 7+ agents | Add T8 (conformance), T9 (migration) |
+
+### Quick Wins for Idle Agents
+
+These tasks can be completed in <1 day during wait periods:
+
+| Task | Gap | Effort | Impact |
+|------|-----|--------|--------|
+| G11 I/O adapters (ReaderStream, StreamReader) | G11 | <4 hours | Closes P2 gap |
+| G8 convenience ops (symlink, copy, canonicalize) | G8 partial | <1 day | Reduces P2 gap |
+| G12 Windows signal handler | G12 | 1-2 days | Closes P3 gap |

+## 6. Risk Mitigations
+
+| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
+|------|-------------|--------|------------|
+| G3 interop infeasible (Tokio Context thread-local) | Medium | Critical | Early spike (T7.2), fallback to per-crate shims |
+| QUIC implementation exceeds 8 weeks | Medium | High | Scope to connection+stream MVP, defer 0-RTT |
+| H2 compile errors block CI | High | Medium | Already tracked; agent in-flight fix |
+| Beads DB contention during multi-agent work | High | Low | Use `--no-db`, coordinate via agent mail |
+| Evidence collection bottleneck at Phase E | Medium | Medium | Start E6/E7 docs incrementally in each phase |

+
+### Per-Track Status Template
+
+Agents should update track status using this format in agent mail:
+
+```
+Track: T<N>
+Phase: A/B/C/D/E
+Tasks closed: X / Y
+Current task: T<N>.<M>
+Blockers: [list or "none"]
+Next milestone: M<K>
+```
+
+### bv Triage Integration
+
+This roadmap is the canonical input for `bv --robot-triage`. Track priorities:
+1. Any unblocked task in T7 (interop) — highest priority
+2. Any unblocked task in T4 (QUIC) — second priority
+3. Any unblocked task in T5/T6 — third priority
+4. Quick wins (G11, G8 partial, G12) — opportunistic
+5. All other tracks — standard priority
+
+### Re-Roadmap Triggers
+
+This roadmap should be re-baselined when:
+- M2 (Interop MVP) decision point reached
+- Any R4 risk remains unresolved past Week 8
+- Agent count changes by ≥2 (rebalance allocation)
+- A new critical gap is discovered
